address: 97 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA
identity_notes: Baohaus (Eddie Huang) closed its earlier East 14th St location in 2020. It reopened as 'Baohaus 2.0' at 97 St Marks Pl in early 2026 (per EV Grieve coverage Feb/Mar 2026 and press). Confirmed via official site homepage (baohaus.nyc) and multiple 2026 press pieces that it is currently operating at this exact address.

sources_checked.limitation: Homepage only; used for identity/hours confirmation, not menu content.
Full dinner menu list; dish names and prices only, no ingredient descriptions or allergen icons on this page.
Full lunch menu list; includes explicit disclosure 'All baos contain peanuts, but can be served without by request.' Dish names and prices only otherwise.
Beverage list only (wine, beer, cocktails, zero-proof); no ingredient/allergen notes provided beyond names.

manual_review_reason: Restaurant reopened in early 2026 after a multi-year closure; menu is entirely new ('Baohaus 2.0') vs. the original cult-favorite menu, and site provides no per-dish ingredient descriptions or allergen icons beyond the single peanut disclosure for baos on the lunch page. Several allergen calls (e.g. sha cha/sacha sauce, 'soy bean crisp' toppings, kung pao) rely on inference from dish naming/typical preparation rather than restaurant-stated ingredients — recommend a human spot-check against the physical menu or staff confirmation before relying on this for a diner with severe allergies.
